desc:
Bug 1950946 - Remove #define realloc for graphite2. r=gfx-reviewers,aosmond

Replacing realloc via define breaks libc++19.1.7:
```
In file included from Unified_cpp_gfx_graphite2_src0.cpp:29:
In file included from /build/firefox-135.0.1/gfx/graphite2/src/Collider.cpp:31:
In file included from /build/firefox-135.0.1/objdir/dist/stl_wrappers/functional:62:
In file included from /build/firefox-135.0.1/objdir/dist/system_wrappers/functional:3:
In file included from /nix/store/j34khyjjc2i4sxyxfk86bwcxlrz36z6g-libcxx-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu-19.1.7-dev/include/c++/v1/functional:552:
In file included from /nix/store/j34khyjjc2i4sxyxfk86bwcxlrz36z6g-libcxx-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu-19.1.7-dev/include/c++/v1/__functional/boyer_moore_searcher.h:27:
In file included from /build/firefox-135.0.1/objdir/dist/stl_wrappers/vector:62:
In file included from /build/firefox-135.0.1/objdir/dist/system_wrappers/vector:3:
In file included from /nix/store/j34khyjjc2i4sxyxfk86bwcxlrz36z6g-libcxx-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu-19.1.7-dev/include/c++/v1/vector:3023:
In file included from /build/firefox-135.0.1/objdir/dist/system_wrappers/locale:3:
/nix/store/j34khyjjc2i4sxyxfk86bwcxlrz36z6g-libcxx-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu-19.1.7-dev/include/c++/v1/locale:2458:26: error: no member named 'moz_xrealloc' in namespace 'std'
 2458 |   _Tp* __t       = (_Tp*)std::realloc(__owns ? __b.get() : 0, __new_cap);
      |                          ^~~~~
/build/firefox-135.0.1/objdir/dist/include/mozilla/mozalloc.h:82:16: note: 'moz_xrealloc' declared here
   82 | MFBT_API void* moz_xrealloc(void* ptr, size_t size) MOZ_INFALLIBLE_ALLOCATOR;
```

We no longer support GCC 5.4, so we shouldn't need this.

* Various events handlers to set the state of the toolbar-context-menu popup,
* as well as to handle some commands from that popup.
* Makes visible the "autohide the downloads button" checkbox in the popup
* in the event that the downloads button was context clicked. Otherwise,
* This method also sets the checkbox state depending on the current user
* configuration for hiding the downloads button.
*   The toolbar-context-menu element for a window.
* Handler for the toolbar-context-autohide-downloads-button command event
* that is fired when the checkbox for autohiding the downloads button is
* changed. This method does the work of updating the internal preference
* state for auto-hiding the downloads button.
* Makes visible the "always open downloads panel" checkbox in the popup
* in the event that the downloads button was context clicked. Otherwise,
* This method also sets the checkbox state depending on the current user
* configuration for always showing the panel.
*   The toolbar-context-menu element for a window.
* that is fired when the checkbox for always showing the downloads panel is
* changed. This method does the work of updating the internal preference
* state for always showing the downloads panel.
* This is called when a menupopup for configuring toolbars fires its
* popupshowing event. There are multiple such menupopups, and this logic
* tries to work for all of them. This method will insert menuitems into
* the popup to allow for controlling the toolbars within the browser
*   The popupshowing event for the menupopup.
*   The point within the menupopup to insert the controls for each toolbar.
// triggerNode can be a nested child element of a toolbaritem.
parent.getAttribute("overflowfortoolbar") || // Needs to work in the overflow list as well.
// Show/hide fullscreen context menu items and set the
// autohide item's checked state to mirror the autohide pref.
// View -> Toolbars menu doesn't have the moveToPanel or removeFromToolbar items.
// The "normal" toolbar items menu separator is hidden because it's unused
// when hiding the "moveToPanel" and "removeFromToolbar" items on flexible
// space items. But we need to ensure its hidden state is reset in the case
// the context menu is subsequently opened on a non-flexible space item.
* Given an opened menupopup, returns the triggerNode that opened that
* menupopup. If customize mode is enabled, this will return the unwrapped
* underlying triggerNode, rather than the customize mode wrapper around it.
*   The menupopup to get the unwrapped trigger node for.
*   The underlying trigger node that opened the menupopup.
// Toolbar buttons are wrapped in customize mode. Unwrap if necessary.
* For an opened menupopup, if the triggerNode was provided by an extension,
* returns the extension ID. Otherwise, return the empty string.
*   The ID of the extension that provided the triggerNode, or the empty
*   string if the triggerNode was not provided by an extension.
* For an opened menupopup, if the triggerNode was provided by an extension,
* returns the widget ID of the triggerNode. Otherwise, return the empty
*   The ID of the extension-provided widget that was the triggerNode, or the
*   empty string if the trigger node was not provided by an extension
* Updates the toolbar context menu to show the right state if an
* extension-provided widget acted as the triggerNode. This will, for example,
* show or hide items for managing the underlying addon.
*   The menupopup for the toolbar context menu.
*   The popupshowing event for the menupopup.
*   Resolves once the menupopup state has been set.
* Handler for the context menu item for removing an extension.
*   The menupopup that triggered the extension removal.
*   Resolves when the extension has been removed.
* Handler for the context menu item for issuing a report on an extension.
*   The menupopup that triggered the extension report.
*   A string describing the UI entrypoint for the report.
*   Resolves when the extension has been removed.
* Handler for the context menu item for managing an extension.
*   The menupopup that triggered extension management.

lines.line: /* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
* License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
* file, You can obtain one at http://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */
// Right-click on the stop/reload button should
// show a context menu with options to move it.
// Right-click on the sidebar button should show a context
// menu with options to toggle vertical tabs and customize.
// Right-click on an empty bit of tabstrip should
// show a context menu without options to move it,
// ensure there are tabs to reload/bookmark:
// Right-click on the title bar spacer before the tabstrip should show a
// context menu without options to move it and no tab-specific options.
info("Skipping test that requires tabs in the title bar.");
// Right-click on an empty bit of extra toolbar should
// show a context menu with moving options disabled,
// and a toggle option for the extra toolbar
// show a context menu with disabled options to move it.
// This clicks in the urlbar container margin, to avoid hitting the urlbar field.
// Right-click on the searchbar and moving it to the menu
// and back should move the search-container instead.
// This is specifically testing the addToPanel function for the search bar, so
// we have to move it to its correct position in the navigation toolbar first.
// The preference will be restored when the customizations are reset later.
// This fails if the screen resolution is small and the search bar overflows
// Right-click on an item within the panel should
// show a context menu with options to move it.
is(overflowPanel.state, "open", "The overflow panel should still be open.");
// Right-click on the stop/reload button while in customization mode
// should show a context menu with options to move it.
// Right-click on an item in the palette should
// show a context menu with options to move it.
// Right-click on an item in the panel while in customization mode
// should show a context menu with options to move it.
// Test the toolbarbutton panel context menu in customization mode
// without opening the panel before customization mode
// Bug 945191 - Combined buttons show wrong context menu options
// when they are in the toolbar.
// Execute the command to move the item from the panel to the toolbar.
// Bug 947586 - After customization, panel items show wrong context menu options
info("Check panel context menu is correct after customization");
is(overflowPanel.state, "open", "The panel should still be open.");
// Bug 982027 - moving icon around removes custom context menu.
"Should have context menu when added to the toolbar."
"Should not have own context menu in the toolbar now that we're customizing."
"Should keep own context menu wrapped when in toolbar."
"Should not have own context menu when in the panel."
"Should keep own context menu wrapped now that we're in the panel."
"Should not have own context menu when back in toolbar because we're still customizing."
"Should keep own context menu wrapped now that we're back in the toolbar."
"Should have context menu again now that we're out of customize mode."
"Should be in default state after removing button."
// Bug 1690575 - 'pin to overflow menu' and 'remove from toolbar' should be hidden
ok(!lastSpring.parentNode, "Spring should have been removed successfully.");

// NOTE: CustomTitlebar behaviour isn't under test here. We just want to assert on
// the right stuff being visible whatever the case for the given window.
"Tabstrip is before the new tab button"
// flip the pref to move the tabstrip into the sidebar
"Tabstrip is slotted into the sidebar vertical tabs container"
// make sure the tab context menu still works
info("Open a new tab using the context menu.");
info("Pin a tab using the context menu.");
"Container should extend far beyond the last tab."
// We are testing click events on the tabstrip itself, rather than on any
// specific button. Disable a11y checks for this portion.
// Synthesize a double click 100px below the last tab
// Middle click should also open a new tab.
"Bookmarks menu item should not be present"
"Undo close tab item should be visible"
info("Open a new tab using the new tab button.");
// Middle click on new tab button should also open a new tab.
info("Open a new tab middle clicking the new tab button.");
// Make sure there is something in the clipboard that can be opened.
// flip the pref to move the tabstrip horizontally
"Tabstrip is before the new tab button"
"sidebar.verticalTabs pref is enabled after we've enabled it."
"sidebar.revamp pref is also enabled after we've enabled vertical tabs."
// use Session restore to batch-open tabs
info("Windows and tabs opened, waiting for readyWindowsPromise");
info("Open a new tab using the new tab button.");
"New tab button is visible while tabs are overflowing."
// We expect the launcher to be expanded by default when enabling vertical tabs
// We expect the launcher to be expanded by default when enabling vertical tabs
"Tab min-width is set to 'auto' when vertical tabs are enabled."
"Tab min-width is set based on the browser.tabs.tabMinWidth pref in horizontal tabs mode."
